involved 600 black men 399 with syphilis
and 201 without who in exchange for
being part of the study were told that
they would get adequate treatment but
that didn't happen even when penicillin
became the go-to
in 1947 researchers didn't offer it to
the participants or give them a chance
to quit the study which lasted for
another 25 years many men died and some

understatement
to prevent another study like Tuskegee
the government passed the National
